options.content: The system is completely state controllable for any nonzero values of p and q.
Only p =0 and  q=0  result in controllability.
The system is uncontrollable for all values of p and q.
We cannot conclude about controllability from the given data.

content: A linear system is equivalently represented by two sets of state equations. $$\mathop x\limits^ \bullet   = \,\,{\rm A}X\,\, + BU$$ and $$\mathop W\limits^ \bullet   = \,\,CW\,\, + DU.$$ The eigen values of the representations are also computed as $$\left[ \lambda  \right]\,\,\,\,and\,\,\,\,\left[ \mu  \right].$$ Which one of the following statements is true?

The transfer function Y(s)/U(s) of a system described by the state equations
$$\mathop x\limits^ \bullet $$(t) = -2x(t)+2u(t)
y(t) = 0.5x(t) is

Consider a system where $x_1(t), x_2(t)$, and $x_3(t)$ are three internal state signals and $u(t)$ is the input signal. The differential equations governing the system are given by

$$ \frac{d}{d t}\left[\begin{array}{l} x_1(t) \\ x_2(t) \\ x_3(t) \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x_1(t) \\ x_2(t) \\ x_3(t) \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right] u(t) $$

Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

options.content: The signals $x_1(t), x_2(t)$, and $x_3(t)$ are bounded for all bounded inputs.
There exists a bounded input such that at least one of the signals $x_1(t), x_2(t)$, and $x_3(t)$ is unbounded.
There exists a bounded input such that the signals $x_1(t), x_2(t)$ and $x_3(t)$ are unbounded.
The signals $x_1(t), x_2(t)$ and $x_3(t)$ are unbounded for all bounded inputs.

explanation:
From the given matrix, the eigen values are $2,-2,0$

Hence system $x_1(t)$ whose eigen value is positive, it is unbounded for bounded input.

Similarly system $x_3(t)$ is also unbounded for bounded input.

Only system $x_2(t)$ is bounded for bounded input (since its eigen value is negative).

content:
Consider a system $S$ represented in state space as

$$\frac{dx}{dt} = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 \end{bmatrix}x + \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 0 \end{bmatrix}r , \quad y = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & -5 \end{bmatrix}x.$$

Which of the state space representations given below has/have the same transfer function as that of $S$?
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Given : State space model of system } S\\ &\begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d t}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 \end{array}\right] x+\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] r, y=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & -5 \end{array}\right] x \\ \text { i.e. } \quad \dot{x} & =\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 \end{array}\right] x+\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] r, \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)\\ y & =\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & -5 \end{array}\right] x \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)\end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { State equation is given by }\\ &\dot{x}=A x+B u \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iii)\end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Output state equation is given by }\\ &y=C x+D u \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iv)\end{aligned} $$

On comparing equation (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv)

$$ \begin{aligned} & A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right], C=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & -5 \end{array}\right] \\ & D=0 \end{aligned} $$

Transfer function is given by

$$ T(s)=C[s I-A]^{-1} B+D \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(v)$$

Substituting all the values of $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ and D in equation (v)

$$ T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} s & 2 \\ -1 & s+3 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]+0 $$

$$\eqalign{ & T(s) = [2\,\,\, - 5]\underline {\left[ \matrix{ s + 3\,\,\,\, - 1 \hfill \cr 1\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s\, \hfill \cr} \right]} \left[ \matrix{ 1 \hfill \cr 0 \hfill \cr} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \cr & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s(s + 3) + 2 \cr & T(s) = [2\,\,\, - 5]\underline {\left[ \matrix{ s + 3\,\,\,\, - 2 \hfill \cr 1\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s\, \hfill \cr} \right]} \left[ \matrix{ 1 \hfill \cr 0 \hfill \cr} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \cr & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{s^2} + 3s + 2 \cr} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & T(\mathrm{~s})=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} \frac{s+3}{s^2+3 s+2} & \frac{-2}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ \frac{1}{s^2+3 s+2} & \frac{s}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] \\ & T(\mathrm{~s})=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 2 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \frac{s+3}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ \frac{1}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\frac{2(s+3)+(-5)}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ & T(\mathrm{~s})=\frac{2 s+6-5}{s^2+3 s+2}=\frac{2 s+1}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the transfer function of given state space model is

$$ T(s)=\frac{2 s+1}{s^2+3 s+2}=\frac{1+2 s}{s^2+3 s+2} $$

Checking from options:

From option (A) :

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d t}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 1 \\ -2 & -3 \end{array}\right] x+\left[\begin{array}{l} 0 \\ 1 \end{array}\right] r, y=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right] x \\ & \text { Here, } A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 1 \\ -2 & -3 \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 0 \\ 1 \end{array}\right], C=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right], D=0 \end{aligned} $$

Transfer function is given by,

$$ \begin{aligned} & T(s)=C[s I-A]^{-1} B+D \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} s & -1 \\ 2 & s+3 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 0 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]+0 \end{aligned} $$

$$\eqalign{ & T(s) = [1\,\,\,2]\underline {\left[ \matrix{ s + 3\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,1 \hfill \cr - 2\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s\, \hfill \cr} \right]} \left[ \matrix{ 0 \hfill \cr 1 \hfill \cr} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \cr & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s(s + 3) + 2 \cr & T(s) = [1\,\,\,\,\,2]\underline {\left[ \matrix{ s + 3\,\,\,\,\,\,1 \hfill \cr - 2\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s\, \hfill \cr} \right]} \left[ \matrix{ 0 \hfill \cr 1 \hfill \cr} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \cr & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{s^2} + 3s + 2 \cr} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} \frac{s+3}{s^3+3 s+2} & \frac{1}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ \frac{-2}{s^2+3 s+2} & \frac{s}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 0 \\ 1 \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \frac{1}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ \frac{s}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\frac{1+2 s}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{aligned} $$

Hence option (A) is correct.

From option (B) :

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d t}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 1 \\ -2 & -3 \end{array}\right] x+\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] r, y=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \end{array}\right] x \\ & \text { Here, } A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 1 \\ -2 & -3 \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right], C=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \end{array}\right], D=0 \\ & T(s)=C[s I-A]^{-1} B+D \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} s & -1 \\ 2 & s+3 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]+0 \end{aligned} $$

$$\eqalign{ & T(s) = [0\,\,\,2]\underline {\left[ \matrix{ s + 3\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,1 \hfill \cr - 2\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s\, \hfill \cr} \right]} \left[ \matrix{ 0 \hfill \cr 1 \hfill \cr} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \cr & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s(s + 3) + 2 \cr} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & T(\mathrm{~s})=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} \frac{s+3}{s^2+3 s+2} & \frac{1}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ \frac{-2}{s^2+3 s+2} & \frac{s}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] \\ & T(\mathrm{~s})=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \frac{s+3}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ \frac{-2}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\frac{-4}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Hence, option (B) is not correct. } \end{aligned} $$

From option (C) :

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d t}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 \end{array}\right] x+\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right] r, y=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \end{array}\right] x \\ & \text { Here, } A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 \end{array}\right] B=\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right], C=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \end{array}\right], D=0 \end{aligned} $$

Transfer function is given by

$$ \begin{aligned} & T(s)=C[s I-A]^{-1} B+D \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} s+1 & 0 \\ 0 & s+2 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]+0 \end{aligned} $$

$$\eqalign{ & T(s) = [1\,\,\,1]\underline {\left[ \matrix{ s + 2\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,0 \hfill \cr \,\,0\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,s + 1\, \hfill \cr} \right]} \left[ \matrix{ - 1 \hfill \cr \,\,3 \hfill \cr} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \cr & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(s + 1)(s + 2) \cr} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} \frac{(s+2)}{(s+1)(s+2)} & 0 \\ 0 & \frac{(s+1)}{(s+1)(s+2)} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{-1}{(s+1)} \\ \frac{3}{(s+2)} \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\frac{-1}{(s+1)}+\frac{3}{(s+2)} \\ & T(s)=\frac{-s-2+3 s+3}{(s+1)(s+2)} \\ & T(s)=\frac{(2 s+1)}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Hence option (C) is correct. } \end{aligned} $$

From option (D) :

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d t}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 \end{array}\right] x+\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right] r, y=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right] x \\ & \text { Here, } A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right], C=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right], D=0 \end{aligned} $$

Transfer function is given by,

$$ T(s)=C[s I-A]^{-1} B+D $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} s+1 & 0 \\ 0 & s+2 \end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]+0 \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} \frac{(s+2)}{(s+1)(s+2)} & 0 \\ 0 & \frac{(s+1)}{(s+1)(s+2)} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{(s+1)} \\ \frac{1}{(s+2)} \end{array}\right] \\ & T(s)=\frac{s+2+2(s+1)}{s^2+3 s+2} \\ & T(s)=\frac{3 s+3}{s^2+3 s+2} \end{aligned} $$

So, option (D) is not correct.

Hence the correct options are (A) and (C).

content:
The electrical system shown in the figure converts input source current $i_s(t)$ to output voltage $\theta_O(t)$.

Current $i_L(t)$ in the inductor and voltage $\vartheta_C(t)$ across the capacitor ate taken as the state variables, both assumed to be initially equal to Zero, i.e., $i_L(0)=0$ and $\vartheta_c(0)=0$. The system is

options.content: completely state controllable as well as completely observable

explanation:
From the above question we can state the following formulas for the given data

$$ V_L=L \frac{d i_L}{d t} \text { and } I_C=C \frac{d V_c}{d t} $$

Also,  $$ -i_s+i_c+\frac{V_o}{1}=0 $$

or,  $$ i_s=i_c+V_o $$

or,  $$ i_c=-V_o-i_s=-V_C-i_s $$

$$ \dot{V}_C=\frac{d V_c}{d t}=\frac{-1}{c}\left[V_c+i_s\right] \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$$

Then we have

$$ \begin{aligned} i_s & =i_L+i_R \\ \text { or, } \quad i_s & =i_L+\frac{V_L}{1} \\ \text { or, } \quad i_s & =i_L+L \cdot \frac{d_{i L}}{d t} \\ L \cdot \frac{d_{i L}}{d t} & =i_s-i_L \\We\,\, get\,\, \dot{I}_L=\frac{d_{i L}}{d t} & =\frac{-1}{L} i_L+\frac{-1}{L} i_s \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)\end{aligned} $$

From above equation (i) and equation (ii)

$$ \begin{aligned}We \,\,have\,\, {\left[\begin{array}{c} \dot{I}_L \\ \dot{V}_C \end{array}\right] } & =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -\frac{1}{L} & 0 \\ 0 & -\frac{1}{C} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} I_L \\ V_C \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{L} \\ -\frac{1}{C} \end{array}\right] i_s \\ {\left[\begin{array}{c} \dot{I}_L \\ \dot{V}_C \end{array}\right] } & =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} I_L \\ V_C \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \end{array}\right] i_s \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iii)\\ V_o & =V_c \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(iv)\end{aligned} $$

Form above mentioned equations, we have

$$ \begin{aligned} & A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right] \text { also } B=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \end{array}\right] \\ & C=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 1 \end{array}\right] \text { and } D=0 \end{aligned} $$

Now we have, Controllability $Q_c$ as $=\left[\begin{array}{ll}\mathrm{B} & \mathrm{AB}\end{array}\right]$

Now we have the situation that if $\left|Q_c\right| \neq 0 \Rightarrow$ here system is controllable else it is otherwise uncontrollable observability $Q_0=\left[\begin{array}{ll}C^T & A^T C^T\end{array}\right]$

For observability, we have the condition that $\left|Q_0\right| \neq 0$

$$ \text { i.e., } \quad Q_c=\left[\begin{array}{ll} B & A B \end{array}\right] $$

$$ \begin{aligned}or,\,\,\,\,\,\, A B & =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \end{array}\right] \downarrow \\ & =\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \end{array}\right] \\Also,\,\, Q_c & =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -1 \\ -1 & 1 \end{array}\right] \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned}Then\,\,\, \left|Q_c\right| & =0 \\ Q_o & =\left[\begin{array}{ll} C^T & A^T C^T \end{array}\right] \end{aligned} $$

Now proceeding from above we have

$$ \begin{aligned} A^T C^T & =\left[\begin{array}{cc} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 0 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -1 \end{array}\right] \\ Q_o & =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 0 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right] \\ \text { or, } \quad\left|Q_c\right| & =0 \end{aligned} $$

$\therefore$ We can say that the system is neither controllable nor observable

the transfer function H(s)$$\left[ { = {{Y\left( s \right)} \over {U\left( s \right)}}} \right]is$$

content:
A linear second order single input continuous-time system is described by the following set of differential equations $$$\eqalign{ & \mathop {{x_1}}\limits^ \bullet \left( t \right) = - 2{x_1}\left( t \right) + 4{x_2}\left( t \right) \cr & \mathop {{x_1}}\limits^ \bullet \left( t \right) = 2{x_1}\left( t \right) - {x_2}\left( t \right) + u\left( t \right) \cr} $$$
Where x1(t) and x2(t) are the state variables and u (t) is the control variable. The system is

content:
For the circuit shown in the figure, choose state variables as $${x_{1,}}{x_{2,}}{x_3}$$ to be $${i_{L1}}\left( t \right),{v_{c2}}\left( t \right),{i_{L3}}\left( t \right)$$

Wriote the state equations

$$$\left[ {\matrix{ {\mathop {{x_1}}\limits^ \bullet } \cr {\mathop {{x_2}}\limits^ \bullet } \cr {\mathop {{x_3}}\limits^ \bullet } \cr } } \right] = A\left[ {\matrix{ {{x_1}} \cr {{x_2}} \cr {{x_3}} \cr } } \right] + B\left[ {e\left( t \right)} \right]$$$

Obtain a state space representation in diagonal form for the following system
$$${{{d^3}y} \over {d{t^3}}} + 6{{{d^2}y} \over {d{t^2}}} + 11{{dy} \over {dt}} + 6y = 6u\left( t \right)$$$

A system is characterized by the following state-space equations:

a) Find the transfer function of the system

b) Compute the state-transition matrix/p>

a) Solve the state equation for a unit-step input under zero initial conditions
answer:
a) $$T.F = {1 \over {\left( {s + 1} \right)\left( {s + 2} \right)}}$$

b) State transition matrix

$$$\left[ {\matrix{ { - {e^{ - t}} + 2{e^{ - 2t}}} & {{e^{ - t}} - {e^{ - 2t}}} \cr { - 2{e^{ - t}} + 2{e^{ - 2t}}} & {2{e^{ - t}} - {e^{ - 2t}}} \cr } } \right]$$$

c) State Equation
$$$\left[ {\matrix{ {{1 \over 2}u\left( t \right) - {e^{ - t}} + {1 \over 2}{e^{ - 2t}}} \cr {{3 \over 2}u\left( t \right) - 2{e^{ - t}} + {1 \over 2}{e^{ - 2t}}} \cr } } \right]$$$
